assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Atomic recreation of ALL slices' and emphasizes 'This is NOT a rolling upgrade' with comparison table showing the difference from rolling GPU maintenance

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Clearly states 'A maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in your multislice environment. Even if only one slice needs maintenance, your entire 4-slice training job will restart.'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'Complete training interruption: Your LLM training job will be forcibly terminated' and 'all 4 TPU v5e slices (1,024 total chips) will be recreated simultaneously'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: States 'If insufficient TPU v5e capacity exists during recreation... your slices may be blocked until hardware becomes available. This could extend downtime from minutes to hours or longer.'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Includes 'Force a fresh checkpoint' in the preparation checklist and provides specific code example for triggering immediate checkpoint before maintenance

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Includes 'Training script handles checkpoint resumption gracefully' in checklist and shows 'Restart training from checkpoint' procedures with specific commands

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer the maintenance event

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response mentions 'expected maintenance window' and preparation timelines, it does not discuss controlling or scheduling when the maintenance occurs or planning breaks around it
